title: Norwegian Turkish Delight Pastry
description: A salty, flaky, and light Norwegian pastry featuring Turkish delight and coconut cream crack, low in carbs and perfect for a snack or dessert. This pastry is a unique fusion of Norwegian and Turkish flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine almond flour and cold unsalted butter, using a pastry blender to work the butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Press the mixture into a disk, wrap in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a small bowl, mix together chopped Turkish delight, coconut cream, erythritol, and vanilla extract.
Spread the Turkish delight mixture evenly over the center of the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the egg wash over the pastry to give it a golden brown color.
Sprinkle shredded coconut over the top of the pastry.
Bake the pastry for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
Allow the pastry to cool completely before serving.
